title: Avatar: Fire and Ash Reaches Top 30 All Time
text: James Cameron's Avatar: Fire and Ash continues its box office dominance, claiming the #1 position for its fourth consecutive weekend with $21.3 million in earnings. The film has accumulated $342.5 million domestically over 24 days, positioning it within the top tier of all-time earners. As reported, it now ranks 41st all time behind Toy Story 4 ($346.5 million) and Harry Potter and the Deathly Hallows Part 2 ($343.0 million). While the film shows strong performance, reaching the $400 million domestic threshold remains uncertain. Its fourth-weekend haul of $21.3 million ranks 42nd on the 24-day chart. However, analysis suggests potential: 19 had a lower fourth go than Fire and Ash and still reached the milestone. Despite domestic questions, the film's international performance has been exceptional. The sequel has earned over $1.23 billion globally, securing a position among the top 30 highest-grossing films worldwide—a testament to the franchise's enduring appeal across markets.
